data.description: Fed bids for 7-year notes total $5.3 bln.

data.title: LegalOn hires Vanessa Butnick Davis as Chief Product Officer to lead global product vision and strategy.
data.description: LegalOn Technologies, a prominent legal AI company, has recently appointed Vanessa Butnick Davis as their Chief Product Officer. In her new position, Davis will oversee the company's worldwide product vision, strategy, and roadmap, with a focus on creating advanced legal AI solutions tailored to the changing requirements of legal teams across the globe. With extensive experience in legal technology and a successful history of expanding customer-centric innovations, Davis is expected to drive the company's growth and development in the industry.

data.title: New report by US Payments Forum explores AI, 5G, IoT impact on contextual payments, could boost global e-commerce revenue by $240 billion.
data.description: The U.S. Payments Forum released a white paper titled "Contextual Payments Considering Artificial Intelligence, 5G, and Internet of Things," discussing how the combination of these technologies is changing commerce and payment processes. Contextual payments enable automatic transactions without traditional checkout procedures, utilizing stored payment details, contextual triggers, and prior consent. This advancement fuels agentic commerce where AI agents make purchases for users, potentially boosting global e-commerce conversion by 1.5–2.5% annually, leading to over $240 billion in extra retail revenue. The white paper explores how AI, 5G, and IoT together facilitate contextual payments in various sectors like retail, mobility, smart homes, healthcare, entertainment, infrastructure, and business services.

data.title: Trump fires Fed Gov. Lisa Cook for 'false statements' on mortgage agreements; alleges deceitful conduct.
data.description: Lisa Cook, a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, was dismissed by President Donald Trump on Monday. Trump claimed Cook made false statements on mortgage agreements, resulting in her immediate removal from the board. In a letter posted on Truth Social, Trump referenced his authority under the Constitution and the Federal Reserve Act of 1913 for Cook's ousting. The decision was based on allegations of deceptive and potentially illegal behavior, stemming from a criminal referral by William J. Pulte, director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, to U.S. Attorney General Pamela Bondi. For more details, you can find the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Indy Pass adds 43 new resorts across 10 states and 14 countries for 2025/2026 season, making a total of 271 resorts available. Limited passes back on sale.
data.description: In Granby, Colorado, Indy Pass revealed that 43 additional resorts have enlisted for the 2025/2026 season, adding to the 228 resorts returning from the previous year. This marks a record-breaking coalition of 271 resorts. Due to the expanded capacity from these new partnerships, a restricted number of Indy Passes are now available for purchase by the general public, with only limited quantities offered. The pass sales are closely regulated by Indy Pass to maintain the distinctive offerings from its independent resorts.

data.title: Stellar (XLM) expected to reach $0.48-0.57 by Sept, with bullish signals and analyst consensus.
data.description: Analysts foresee a positive trend for XLM, predicting a price increase to the range of $0.48 to $0.57 in the next month. Stellar is nearing oversold levels, further fueling expectations of a bullish trajectory. This optimistic forecast is backed by a strong consensus among analysts.

data.title: DealStream's Q3 2025 survey shows tight financing market; buyers have upper hand in M&A deals.
data.description: In Boston, the Q3 2025 “Main Street” M&A report by DealStream is now available for review. This report provides insights into the expectations of buyers, sellers, and brokers regarding prices, deal flow, and financing. Stay informed about the latest trends in the market with this comprehensive report.

data.title: Snowflake's stock jumped after beating Q2 earnings and revenue targets, raising outlook; October quarter product revenue above views.
data.description: Stock of Snowflake saw a surge as a result of its Q2 earnings and revenue, which surpassed Wall Street's expectations. The company also provided a positive outlook for its product revenue in the October quarter, exceeding projections. This growth was attributed to data modernization driving AI projects, with the company witnessing significant progress in this area.

data.title: Jack in the Box marks 10 years supporting No Kid Hungry, raising over $6 million to fight childhood hunger.
data.description: In San Diego, Jack in the Box (NASDAQ: JACK) commemorates their 10-year partnership with No Kid Hungry, showcasing a steadfast dedication to eradicating childhood hunger in the U.S. Over the past six years, Jack in the Box's franchisees, employees, and customers have collectively raised more than $6 million. These funds support initiatives that ensure children receive essential, nourishing meals for proper development. For Hunger Action Month this September, Jack in the Box encourages guests to join their ongoing efforts.

data.title: Ninepoint Partners launches 11 Canadian Single Stock ETFs on TSX, providing investors access to Ninepoint HighShares ETFs.
data.description: On August 22, 2025, Ninepoint Partners LP, a prominent independent investment manager in Canada, revealed the introduction of eleven fresh ETFs on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX). This move enables Canadian investors to utilize Ninepoint HighShares ETFs with the recently listed tickers on the exchange.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures up before Fed Chief's speech; Nvidia to stop AI chip. Market awaits Powell's address.
data.description: As Fed chief Jerome Powell prepares to give a speech at Jackson Hole on Friday morning, the market indexes and top stocks are at a crucial point. Nvidia has allegedly stopped production of H20, impacting the AI chip sector. Dow Jones futures are on the rise ahead of Powell's speech, indicating optimism among investors. This development is significant as it can influence market movements in the near future.

data.title: Wall St. traders preparing for tech market crash as QQQ ETF shows warning signs, with put skew nearing 3-year high.
data.description: Wall Street traders are increasingly buying bearish options anticipating a potential tech meltdown rather than a mild correction in the market. The Invesco QQQ Trust, which tracks the Nasdaq 100 index, has surged 43% from April's lows, but traders are now betting on sharp downside movements using deep out-of-the-money puts. This suggests growing concerns about a sudden tech plunge. The key metric behind this trend is put skew, measuring the demand for crash insurance compared to milder protection. The 2-month put skew for QQQ is close to its 3-year highs, indicating a rise in tail risk pricing. Institutional investors are taking note of this as tech stock gains have been significant this summer. This shift in options trading behavior is a potential signal that the market's recent rally might be facing challenges.

data.title: eClerx earns top spot in Everest Group's PEAK Matrix® 2025 for Capital Markets Operations, named Leader & Star Performer.
data.description: eClerx, a company based in New York, has been acknowledged as a top performer in Everest Group's Capital Markets Operations Services PEAK Matrix® for 2025. The recognition highlights their leadership and exceptional performance in the industry. This recognition comes as eClerx celebrates its 25th year in the business.
